title: FHS 215 — Criminal & Juvenile Justice Course Notes (Lindsey Nichols, Fall 2020)
html:
The full 1.1MB transcript of Yahir's FHS 215 (Criminal & Juvenile Justice) course taught by Lindsey Nichols in Fall 2020 — captured Jan 17 2021 as a single Google Doc. Spans every lecture deck: justice-system terminology, history of policing, bail/probation/parole mechanics, juvenile justice, sentencing disparities, mass incarceration in the Reagan era "tough on crime" period, Harmony Academy (Oregon's first Recovery High School) as an intervention model, and the protective/risk factors framework around adolescent substance use.

TL;DR

  • Comprehensive Fall-2020 OSU dump from FHS 215 with lindsey nichols — the academic backbone for the Al Forthan research essays Yahir wrote two weeks later.
  • Anchors the "addiction is a health problem, not a crime" policy frame Yahir keeps returning to in scholarship essays — DARE was ineffective, harmony academy / counseling > incarceration.
  • Documents the reagan era prison-population spike (329K → 627K over 8 years) and links the war on drugs to the racialized incarceration patterns Yahir cites in his Al Forthan race-and-class essay.
  • Lays out the protective-factors framework (familial involvement · prosocial peers · academic efficacy · delayed onset) that becomes Yahir's recommended-policy template.
  • Earliest vault evidence of Yahir's interest in criminal justice policy / adolescent substance use as a serious research domain — not just a personal-narrative topic.

Key quote

"Viewing and applying policy that treats addiction as a health problem instead of a crime…"

Why it matters in the graph

This is the academic source-of-truth behind Yahir's Al Forthan application — the scholarship-essay arc reads as personal-narrative, but the underlying policy fluency comes from this class. It also seeds his later interest in harm reduction and structural critiques of mass incarceration. The course belongs in the same neighborhood as GLBL 463 / Land of Open Graves — both are 2020-era OSU classes that gave Yahir the policy vocabulary he weaves into personal essays.

Open questions

  • Did Yahir pursue any further policy-coursework after FHS 215, or did the scholarship season absorb the energy?
  • Is there a thread from this class to the later crypto / game theory research interest — incentives-as-policy?

Connections

  • 2021 01 31 Copy of Al Forthan Scholarship — the personal-narrative + research essays this course directly fed.
  • 2021 02 21 GLBL 463 ESSAY 3 — sister Fall-2020 OSU class on migration policy.
  • lindsey nichols — the professor.
  • harmony academy · mass incarceration · reagan era · war on drugs · criminal justice policy · adolescent substance use · osu
